items.title: Dollar firms after strong US jobs data, pushes yen through 160 level
items.summary: The dollar strengthened significantly, poised for over a 1% weekly gain following robust U.S. employment figures. Nonfarm payrolls exceeded expectations, boosting the dollar against the yen, which faces pressure near the 160 barrier. Geopolitical tensions and safe-haven demand further supported the dollar's rise.

items.title: India’s changing relationship with gold: From ownership to financial utility
items.summary: No longer is the discussion about whether Indians will continue owning gold; rather, how well they can leverage that gold in India’s journey towards economic success

items.title: D-Street Ends Another Week in the Red Amid Lack of Triggers
items.summary: Indian stock markets closed lower on Friday, marking a second consecutive weekly decline. Investor sentiment turned cautious following the Reserve Bank of India's monetary policy announcement. Analysts suggest the Nifty may trade within a range in the near term. Foreign investors continued their selling spree, while domestic institutions provided support.

items.title: Social media bans on teens risk strengthening Big Tech's grip on the sector, Bluesky exec warns
items.summary: "We're living in a world where it's almost impossible for smaller entrants to come in and build healthier spaces," Bluesky's COO, Rose Wang, told CNBC.

items.title: FPIs get tax relief on gilts, ease of Investment
items.summary: In a strategic step to lure foreign investment, India has eliminated taxes on capital gains and interest for overseas investors in its government bonds. This change, set to take effect on April 1, is part of an ordinance aimed at enhancing the appeal of Indian sovereign bonds and fortifying the strength of the rupee.

items.title: NSE investor accounts cross 26 crore mark as retail participation surges beyond metro cities
items.summary: India's capital markets are rapidly expanding. The National Stock Exchange has crossed 26 crore trading accounts. This growth is driven by digital platforms and simplified processes. Investor participation is increasing across all cities and asset classes. This signifies a deepening of financial inclusion for many Indians.

items.title: Rupee in rhapsody, passes 95 vs USD level at close
items.summary: On Friday, the Indian Rupee made impressive gains, climbing 84 paise against the US Dollar. This upward momentum came after the Reserve Bank of India and the government introduced initiatives to lure foreign investors. Closing at 94.95, the Rupee reached its strongest point in over two months. Analysts are optimistic about increased inflows, which could further enhance the currency's performance.

items.title: "Strengthen aspirations of 140 crore Indians": Rajnath Singh hails India's 7.7% GDP growth in FY 2025-26
items.summary: India's economy achieved a strong 7.7 percent growth in financial year 2025-26. This performance accelerated to 7.8 percent in the fourth quarter. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh highlighted this resilience, attributing it to the 'Reform, Perform, Transform' mantra. India continues to lead as the world's fastest-growing major economy amidst global uncertainty. This growth strengthens aspirations for Viksit Bharat.

items.title: How Gujarat is writing India’s copper self-reliance story
items.summary: India does not have a copper shortage; it has a copper strategy shortage and the window to fix it is narrowing

items.title: Are Rajesh Exports shares a new Gensol Engineering in the making in the Indian stock market?
items.summary: Rajesh Exports has strongly denied wrongdoing. In a clarification issued after the June 3 interim order, the company said SEBI had not recorded any conclusive adverse findings, imposed any penalty or initiated coercive action.

items.title: European shares slip as Mideast tensions linger; tech stocks pause rally
items.summary: European shares edged lower, on track for a weekly dip as Middle East peace efforts remained uncertain. Technology stocks led the declines, pausing after a significant two-month rally. Brent crude prices rose, with diplomatic resolutions appearing unlikely amid ongoing strikes and a fragile Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.

items.title: US stock market crash explained: Why did Nasdaq plunge 4% to log worst day in over a year
items.summary: Wall Street experienced a sharp decline on Friday, led by the Nasdaq's significant fall, as a robust US jobs report fueled concerns about sustained high interest rates from the Federal Reserve. The strong employment figures intensified inflation worries, making rate cuts less likely and potentially increasing the chance of a future hike.

items.title: Bengaluru-Mumbai IndiGo aircraft suffers bird-hit, flight delayed
items.summary: An IndiGo flight from Bengaluru to Mumbai experienced a bird strike on Friday. The aircraft was taxiing on the runway when the incident occurred. The pilot immediately returned to the bay for necessary checks. After inspecting both engines, the Airbus A321Neo was declared safe. The flight eventually departed over an hour later than its scheduled time.

items.title: Oil prices fall on mounting hopes for de-escalation in US-Iran War
items.summary: Oil prices declined Friday as traders perceived a reduced likelihood of renewed conflict between the US and Iran. Despite this, both Brent and WTI crude futures were poised for their first weekly gains in three weeks, influenced by earlier Middle East tensions and limited Strait of Hormuz traffic. However, gains were tempered by unexpected inventory levels and falling demand.

items.title: Earnings growth, end to West Asia war could drive double-digit gains for Nifty: Ashwini Shami, Omniscence Capital
items.summary: Although FY27 margins may see some compression due to inflationary pressures, even moderate single-digit earnings growth, coupled with a market re-rating as the West Asia crisis eases, could result in double-digit returns for the Nifty from current levels, said Ashwini Shami of Omniscience Capital.

items.title: Midcap magic: These 5 midcap mutual funds rallied up to 10% in 2026
items.summary: Several mutual funds from the midcap space have, therefore, delivered up to 10% returns so far this year, shows data from ACE Mutual Fund. Check these top performers, along with their NAV, expense ratio and other key details.

items.title: PRECIOUS-Gold falls about 3% as robust US jobs data cements bets on higher rates
items.summary: Gold prices experienced a significant drop of approximately 3% on Friday, influenced by a stronger-than-expected U.S. jobs report. This data reinforced expectations that the Federal Reserve will maintain higher interest rates for an extended period due to ongoing inflation concerns.

items.title: Wasting China’s solar panel surplus is madness
items.summary: Clean power is within our reach — yet factories sit idle

items.title: Directors’ Deals: BP veteran hives off nearly £2mn in shares ahead of Manifold brouhaha
items.summary: The group has been struggling to convince the market that it now possesses greater strategic clarity

items.title: Are US Treasuries becoming a financial chokepoint?
items.summary: A haven asset for the financial system is becoming a source of risk

items.title: Bitcoin's star fades, as investors flock to lustre of AI and megacap IPOs
items.summary: Bitcoin is experiencing its worst year-to-date performance in over a decade, with a 15% price drop this week. Capital is shifting towards booming AI stocks and new listings like SpaceX, while Bitcoin's volatility and correlation with other assets have diminished, impacting its appeal as a portfolio diversifier.

items.title: Bitcoin drops below $60,000, a first since October 2024
items.summary: Bitcoin experienced a significant drop below $60,000, its lowest point since October 2024, following a surprise selloff by prominent corporate holder Strategy. This move, despite the minimal amount, rattled market confidence, as the firm was perceived as a consistent accumulator. Analysts suggest long-term investors may see this dip as a buying opportunity amid potential legislative tailwinds.

items.title: AI chip stocks lose $1.3 trillion in value as semiconductor selloff deepens
items.summary: Major AI and chip companies were hit hard, with NVIDIA shedding more than $300 billion in market value, while Micron Technology, Advanced Micro Devices and Marvell Technology posted steep losses.

items.title: Wall Street Week Ahead: Blockbuster SpaceX IPO set to test high-flying US stocks rally
items.summary: SpaceX's massive $75 billion IPO is set to launch next week, potentially valuing the company at $1.75 trillion. Investors are watching closely amid fears of market overexuberance, especially after recent job data fueled concerns about hawkish monetary policy. Key tech earnings and inflation data will also shape market sentiment.

items.title: CMR Green Technologies IPO subscribed 127 times on final day
items.summary: The IPO, priced in the range of ₹182-₹192 per share, is entirely an offer-for-sale of up to 3.28 crore equity shares by promoters and an investor-selling shareholder

items.title: India-US trade deal "75 years overdue"; proposed 12.5% tariff part of talks: USIBC's Atul Keshap
items.summary: US-India Business Council President Atul Keshap believes a trade deal is close. He notes ongoing talks and a US tariff proposal are part of negotiations. Keshap stresses the need for compromise from both sides to finalize the agreement. He urges India to pursue reforms to boost investment. The deal aims to increase bilateral trade significantly.

items.title: Nasdaq suffers biggest drop since April 2025 as chip stocks lead selloff
items.summary: The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite fell 4.18 per cent, marking its steepest one-day decline since April 2025, while the S&P 500 dropped 2.64 per cent and the Dow Jones Industrial Average lost 1.35 per cent.

items.title: Stocks to buy under ₹200: Mehul Kothari of Anand Rathi recommends three shares to buy or sell
items.summary: Kothari recommends MRPL, Reliance Power, and GMR Airports for short-term investments under ₹200. The market remains cautious amid global uncertainties and rising crude oil prices.

items.title: SpaceX’s lofty IPO has serious earthbound problems, from emissions to water
items.summary: Data centers use water primarily for cooling their servers, which process enormous amounts of information.

items.title: SBI MF buys stakes in two Adani Group firms for ₹5,747 crore; GQG Partners exits holdings
items.summary: The transaction follows SBI Mutual Fund’s recent purchase of a stake in Adani Enterprises, signalling continued institutional interest in Adani Group companies.

items.title: Four defence companies cashing in on the BrahMos export surge
items.summary: Here are the defence companies in focus after India’s recently signed BrahMos pact with Vietnam and Indonesia.

items.title: The week in charts: deficit concerns, IIP reset, India’s wedding dreams
items.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.

items.title: Why Reid Hoffman is leaving Microsoft's board for an AI startup?
items.summary: Reid Hoffman steps down from Microsoft board to focus on Manas, his AI drug-discovery startup. Here's what his departure signals about priorities in tech leadership.

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX secures $920 million monthly Google deal for cloud compute capacity- Explained
items.summary: SpaceX lands $920M monthly recurring revenue from Google for AI compute. New infrastructure agreement spans 2.75 years as company approaches IPO filing.

items.title: India defies West Asia war concerns as Q4 GDP growth hits 7.8%; risks remain ahead
items.summary: India's economy surged by a better-than-expected 7.8% in the March quarter, exceeding forecasts and pushing FY26 growth to 7.7%. This robust performance was driven by strong private investment and consumption, defying concerns over the West Asia war. The government remains committed to its reform agenda to maintain economic momentum.

items.title: Solar power curtailment raises red flags for renewable investors
items.summary: The solar capacity that was curtailed on 1 May equals almost one-sixth of the country’s installed capacity of 150GW. Experts said that low demand due to industries closing for Labour Day on 1 May may have contributed to the curtailment.

items.title: A five-year trail of LIC's red flags on Rajesh Exports before Sebi order
items.summary: Years before Sebi flagged alleged revenue inflation at Rajesh Exports, its largest investor LIC’s voting trail shows repeated concerns over disclosures. This highlights the value of public records as investor warning signs, and also raises questions over whether LIC could have done more.

items.title: Central bank turns piper to draw in foreign capital; leaves repo rate at 5.25, keeps stance neutral
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has introduced measures to boost foreign currency inflows, including incentives for external commercial borrowings and bearing hedging costs on FCNR(B) deposits. These steps aim to strengthen external buffers and support the rupee amidst market outflows. The central bank also maintained its policy rate at 5.25% and a neutral stance, while revising inflation and growth forecasts.

items.title: Amazon unveils latest warehouse robot as tech giants continue AI layoffs
items.summary: "Our experience of robots is that it's actually driven up employment rather than the reverse," Amazon executive John Boumphrey told CNBC.

items.title: AstraZeneca CEO says AI is reshaping drug development — and helping boost the odds of success
items.summary: AstraZeneca CEO Pascal Soriot said AI is helping the company develop medicines faster and make smarter decisions throughout the research process.

items.title: Iran's threats against this Red Sea choke point are a big vulnerability for the oil market
items.summary: Oil prices would spike if Iran's Houthi allies started attacking ships passing through the Bab el-Mandeb Strait.

items.title: US stocks slump as fears over Big Tech shake Wall Street
items.summary: The Nasdaq saw its biggest daily fall since early 2025.

items.title: Marvell Technology and Flex to join S&P 500 index, replacing Pool and Campbell's
items.summary: The move highlights the growing importance of the technology sector to the stock market.

items.title: Boeing to start 737 Max production on new assembly line July 6, CEO says
items.summary: The new 737 Max final assembly line in Everett, Washington, will serve as a catalyst for increasing Max production to 52 jets per month.

items.title: Google to pay SpaceX $920 million a month for compute capacity at xAI data centers
items.summary: Ahead of a planned IPO, SpaceX inked a deal to rent compute capacity to Google for $920 million per month for 32 months.

items.title: Bitcoin cracks $60,000, sinking to lowest level since October 2024
items.summary: The weeklong sell-off was exacerbated after a stronger-than-expected May jobs report Friday sent yields higher and pressured risk assets.

items.title: Nasdaq tumbles 4% as shares in chip and memory groups sink
items.summary: Rising expectations of a Federal Reserve rate increase send US bond yields rising sharply

items.title: Mythos rejuvenated the cybersecurity sector. Earnings put the recent rally to the test
items.summary: Mythos gave the cybersecurity sector a boost, but upbeat earnings weren't enough for investors in search of an AI payoff.

items.title: Meta's stock sinks on report company could raise tens of billions of dollars to fund AI push
items.summary: Meta shares dropped after the Financial Times reported the company could potentially raise tens of billions of dollars in a stock offering to help its AI push.

items.title: Remaining three 60 Minutes stars say theyre staying at CBS show, dont want to see it die
items.summary: Remaining three '60 Minutes' stars say they're staying at CBS show, don't want to see it die

items.title: US stocks today: Nasdaq crashes 1,100 pts, Dow 600 pts as chip stocks slide; jobs data fuels rate hike fears
items.summary: Wall Street tumbled as a strong U.S. jobs report dimmed hopes of rate cuts, triggering a sharp selloff in overheated tech and chip stocks. The Nasdaq plunged over 4%, ending a nine-week rally, while persistent Middle East tensions and rising yields further pressured investor sentiment.

items.title: India's forex reserves rise $938 million to $682 billion despite RBI's $5 billion swap
items.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves saw a slight increase of $938 million, reaching $682 billion by May 29. This rise occurred despite a significant dollar-rupee swap by the Reserve Bank of India. The central bank likely sold dollars in the spot market to support the rupee. Foreign currency assets grew, while gold reserves saw a decline.

items.title: FAR expansion, tax relief may boost foreign bond inflows, but risks remain
items.summary: New government debt rules aim to attract foreign investment. Additional government securities are now accessible to foreign investors. Tax exemptions are also in place. These measures are expected to boost Indian debt appeal over time. However, immediate foreign inflows remain uncertain. Global events like oil prices and regional conflicts influence investor sentiment.

items.title: Stocks fall sharply as strong jobs data fuels rate hike bets; oil set for weekly gain
items.summary: GLOBAL-MARKETS:Stocks fall sharply as strong jobs data fuels rate hike bets; oil set for weekly gain

items.title: Bring back export earnings sooner
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has shortened the time for exporters to bring back foreign earnings. This period is now nine months, down from fifteen months. This change aims to increase foreign exchange inflows into India. The move is part of broader efforts to support the country's balance of payments. Exporters must now repatriate earnings sooner after shipment.

items.title: RBI to soon release revised list of upper-layer NBFCs: Governor
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India will soon release an updated list of upper-layer non-banking financial companies. This comes as Tata Sons has requested to be removed from this category. The central bank is examining this request. The new list will be finalised shortly. This update is crucial for financial sector regulations.

items.title: Trump to meet AI leaders to discuss US investment in their companies
items.summary: The US president said on Friday he expects to meet the leaders of top AI companies next week.

items.title: Hindustan Zinc plans Rs 50k-crore capex pipeline for next 5 years
items.summary: Hindustan Zinc will invest ₹40,000-50,000 crore in the next five years. This will double refined metal capacity and ore production. Metal reserves will more than triple. The company has approved new projects costing nearly ₹17,000 crore. This expansion aims to boost production and secure long-term mining operations.

items.title: Trump Says US Still Weighing Share Sale for Fannie, Freddie
items.summary: President Donald Trump said his administration is still considering a public offering of shares in mortgage giants Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac, days after he said he was appointing his top housing official to oversee the government’s network of intelligence agencies.

items.title: Regulator, cos discuss price hike for some cancer drugs
items.summary: India's drug pricing authority may soon approve a price hike for vital platinum-based cancer medicines. These drugs, including carboplatin and cisplatin, are crucial for treating various cancers. The National Pharmaceutical Pricing Authority met with drug makers and hospital representatives to discuss the issue. Rising raw material costs have put pressure on production.

items.title: India's tallest building planned for Gurugram's Global City project
items.summary: India's tallest building is set to rise in Gurugram's upcoming Global City. A 6.7-acre plot has been earmarked for a 620-700 metre mixed-use tower. The Haryana government has consulted developers for this ambitious project near the Dwarka Expressway. This development promises a significant new landmark in the region.

items.title: Free flow of FCNR(B) money: RBI to bear hedging costs on NRI FX deposits, easing banks’ overseas funding
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is now covering all hedging costs for banks raising foreign currency deposits. This move aims to make these deposits more attractive to overseas depositors. Banks expect to offer higher interest rates, potentially up to 100 basis points. This initiative seeks to increase dollar inflows through banking channels and revive interest in FCNR(B) deposits.

items.title: Emerging-Market Currencies Sink on Gangbuster US Jobs Report
items.summary: Currencies in the developing world sank after a blowout US jobs report provided the clearest sign yet that the labor market is breaking out of a prolonged period of lackluster hiring, undercutting the case for rate cuts from the Federal Reserve.

items.title: PSUs to get concessional forex swaps to boost external borrowings
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India will introduce a special foreign exchange swap facility for public sector undertakings. This initiative aims to encourage external commercial borrowings by these entities. The facility will be available until September 30. It is expected to significantly increase dollar inflows into India. Governor Sanjay Malhotra anticipates healthy flows from this and other government measures.

items.title: IT secretary signals stricter govt controls for AI, biometric devices
items.summary: India's IT secretary, S Krishnan, emphasized the need for strategic autonomy and control over AI and biometric hardware. Citing concerns over industrial espionage and data security, he highlighted the importance of sourcing devices from trusted origins, a move already underway in sectors like telecom and CCTV.

items.title: Small packs make big gains as budgets shrink and costs rise
items.summary: With inflation on the rise, Indian shoppers are favoring compact product packaging. This shift not only reflects changing consumer habits but also urges fast-moving consumer goods firms to innovate. In light of increased costs for raw materials, these companies are streamlining their offerings by offering less in each package.

items.title: Gold Falls Most in Two Months as Jobs Fuel Fed Rate-Hike Bets
items.summary: Gold fell the most in more than two months as robust US jobs data fueled bets that the Federal Reserve will likely raise interest rates this year, a headwind for the precious metal.

items.title: Wall Street slumps as chips slide, robust jobs data stokes fears of hawkish Fed
items.summary: USA-STOCKS/ (UPDATE 6, GRAPHIC):US STOCKS-Wall Street slumps as chips slide, robust jobs data stokes fears of hawkish Fed

items.title: U.S. payrolls rose by 172,000 in May, much more than expected; unemployment at 4.3%
items.summary: Nonfarm payrolls were expected to increase by 80,000 in May while the unemployment rate held at 4.3%.

items.title: Trai plan to regulate internet TV triggers pushback from OTT, broadcaster groups
items.summary: In a bid to regulate, India's telecom authority TRAI is exploring potential guidelines for online television services, sparking a backlash from digital firms and broadcasters alike. They worry about the implications of rules that could disproportionately affect OTT platforms and digital news sites, which currently abide by IT regulations without the necessity of licenses.

items.title: For a few dollars more: Inflation bypass can hurt
items.summary: India's central bank holds interest rates steady despite rising inflation. The focus shifts to stabilizing the rupee with a new dollar deposit scheme. This move aims to attract foreign currency and boost confidence. While offering gains to non-resident Indians, it comes at a significant cost and distorts market rates.

items.title: India can increase seafood exports to $30 billion in five years: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India targets $30 billion in seafood exports within five years. This ambitious goal involves increasing production and quality. The nation plans to export more ready-to-eat and branded seafood products. Free trade agreements will enhance market access. The focus is on reducing raw shrimp imports and exporting value-added goods. This strategy aims to significantly boost foreign exchange earnings.

items.title: NTPC eyes low-load thermal units
items.summary: State-owned NTPC Ltd is seeking expressions of interest for developing thermal power units capable of operating at significantly lower loads, down to 25%. This move aims to enhance grid flexibility and support the integration of a growing share of variable renewable energy sources like solar and wind power.

items.title: Model routing is a fix for AI overspending. That's a problem for OpenAI and Anthropic
items.summary: Companies are shifting from running everything on the most powerful AI model to matching each task to the right one, a practice called model routing.

items.title: Govt to tap AI for mapping supply chains and investment clusters
items.summary: India's upcoming Statistical Business Register (SBR) will leverage AI and analytics to map supply chains, identify investment clusters, and guide logistics infrastructure investments. Privacy and consent-based data sharing are central to the framework. The government emphasizes data harmonization as crucial for AI's effectiveness, focusing on semantic interoperability across datasets.

items.title: Oman ports emerge as India's gateway to West Asia, Africa
items.summary: India's CEPA agreement with Oman is officially in motion, turning ports such as Sohar, Salalah, and Duqm into strategic hubs for Indian commerce. These locations pave the way for Indian firms to penetrate the dynamic markets of West and East Africa, as well as North Africa.

items.title: India can regain 7% growth by FY28: Chief Economic Advisor V Anantha Nageswaran
items.summary: India's Chief Economic Advisor V Anantha Nageswaran stated the nation can achieve over 7% growth this fiscal year, driven by policy measures and structural reforms. This projection hinges on a return to pre-February 28 global conditions, as the Iran war has caused economic turmoil.

items.title: India targets near fourfold jump in fisheries exports to $30 billion in 5 years
items.summary: Commerce minister Piyush Goyal noted that seafood exports rose from around $5 billion in 2013-14 to about $8.5 billion in FY26, while global fisheries trade increased from $150 billion to $164 billion during the same period.

items.title: Air India, Riyadh Air sign agreement to expand premium travel options between India, Saudi Arabia
items.summary: India's leading global airline, Air India, and Saudi Arabia's new national carrier, Riyadh Air, have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to establish the foundation for a partnership that will offer guests seamless connections between India, Saudi Arabia, and destinations beyond.

items.title: IDFC First Bank fraud was isolated case involving collusion: KPMG
items.summary: A forensic review has confirmed a Rs 646 crore fraud at IDFC First Bank's Chandigarh branch was an isolated incident. The fraud involved collusion between bank employees, government officials, and third parties. The bank has paid back the principal amount and interest. IDFC First Bank has implemented new controls to prevent future collusion risks at the branch level.

items.title: Apple's WWDC: Tim Cook's AI legacy at stake in his final developer conference as CEO
items.summary: Apple heads into WWDC with Tim Cook’s AI legacy, Siri’s future as an agentic platform, and the stock’s rich valuation all on the line.

items.title: RBI deputy governor Swaminathan J gets two-year extension
items.summary: The union government has extended Reserve Bank of India deputy governor Swaminathan Janakiraman's term for another two years with effect from June 26, 2026, the central bank announced Friday.

items.title: Global tech sell-off spreads to Europe as chip shares fall
items.summary: A sell-off in tech stocks gripped U.S. and Asian markets overnight after an earnings report from Broadcom earlier in the week.

items.title: Bitcoin drops below $60,000, its lowest level since October 2024: Here's why
items.summary: Bitcoin fell below $60,000, its lowest level since October 2024, amid ETF outflows, geopolitical uncertainty, and concerns over corporate crypto treasury demand. Other cryptocurrencies also declined sharply, with Ether falling 12 per cent

items.title: Final pieces fired at Denby as production ends
items.summary: "We are so hugely proud of everything this Derbyshire pottery has achieved," the company says.

items.title: Maize farmers likely to shift to cotton, soybean, paddy, ragi
items.summary: Deviations in rainfall during the June-September monsoon season will have significant implications for agricultural production in India

items.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold tumbles $136/oz; silver sinks $5 as strong US jobs report boosts rate hike bets
items.summary: Precious metals faced selling pressure on 5 June due to a strong US jobs report, potentially prolonging higher interest rates. Gold and silver prices fell sharply, driven by expectations of a rate hike in 2026. International markets mirrored the decline, with gold futures on MCX also plummeting.

items.title: Bond investors bet Colombia’s ‘Tiger’ will slash public spending
items.summary: Rightwing populist Abelardo de la Espriella goes into second-round presidential vote

items.title: FY26 growth higher than anticipated, shows India's resilience: MoSPI Secretary
items.summary: India's GDP growth of 7.7 per cent in FY2025-26 came in higher than anticipated in the Second Advance Estimates (SAE), reflecting the resilience and robustness of the Indian economy despite global headwinds, Secretary, Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI), Saurabh Garg said.

items.title: Lululemon shares drop as forecast cut spotlights challenges for incoming CEO
items.summary: Lululemon faced a notable dip in its stock price as the athleisure retailer adjusted its profit expectations for the year. This news has escalated concerns among investors regarding the company's ability to bounce back. The incoming CEO will face a myriad of challenges, as reports suggest a decline in the brand's overall momentum.

items.title: LinkedIn co-founder Reid Hoffman is leaving Microsoft's board after almost a decade
items.summary: Reid Hoffman was one of OpenAI's first donors, and he left its board in 2023 as the nonprofit cozied up with Microsoft.

items.title: Franklin Resources' Wamco to pay $100 million SEC fine over former star manager's trades
items.summary: Western Asset Management, a unit of Franklin Resources, will pay $100 million to settle SEC charges tied to a $600 million cherry-picking scheme by former CIO Kenneth Leech. The firm did not admit wrongdoing. Leech faces criminal trial, while regulators cited failures in oversight and fair trade allocation practices.

items.title: UP Power Corp receives approval to source power from Bhutan's hydro project: Details
items.summary: Khorluchu Hydro Power is a joint venture between homegrown Tata Power and Druk Green Power Corporation.

items.title: Geopolitical churn continues to impact costs, amid diverse consumer expectations: Nestle chairman Manish Tiwary
items.summary: Nestle India sees rural areas as a major growth avenue. The company is focusing on increasing sales volume and investing more in its brands and production facilities. This strategy aims to navigate changing consumer tastes and economic pressures. Nestle India is also leveraging technology to improve its operations and reach more consumers across the country.Nestle India looks for volume-led growth despite price volatility amid geopolitical uncertainties

items.title: Why SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic IPOs are a pipe dream for Indian investors
items.summary: Retail investors rarely get IPO allocations, as US companies allot shares largely to institutions. The chance of Indians getting allotment is almost nil

items.title: Odds of a Fed hike this year jump on prediction markets
items.summary: Following an unexpectedly hot jobs report, prediction market traders see a 52% chance the Federal Reserve increases interest rates this year.

items.title: Tata Steel UK begins assessment after Port Talbot fire, says Hot Strip Mill restart to begin next week
items.summary: Tata Steel UK said assessment work is underway following a fire at the Pickle Line plant in Port Talbot. No injuries were reported, but a key production line suffered substantial damage. The company aims to restart the Hot Strip Mill next week

items.title: The gold chart looks precarious. Here's how to profit
items.summary: Gold is at a technically precarious juncture, and the good news for you is that options market may be mispricing the risk.

items.title: Hospitality jobs boom as US prepares for World Cup
items.summary: It is the third month in a row US jobs figures have beaten expectations.

items.title: Could you be the FT’s new stock picking champion?
items.summary: Register your portfolio now — the 8-week competition starts on 9 June

items.title: RBI holds rates, rupee surges as markets close flat to negative
items.summary: The BSE Sensex closed at 74,243.34, down 116.67 points, while the Nifty 50 ended at 23,366.70, down 49.85 points

items.title: Spacex IPO is said to draw more orders than shares available
items.summary: SpaceX’s $75 billion IPO is already oversubscribed, signaling strong institutional demand and positioning it to become the largest listing ever. The Elon Musk-led firm may surpass Saudi Aramco’s record. Shares are priced at $135, valuing the company near $1.8 trillion, with trading expected to begin mid-June.

items.title: India’s economy faces threats that currency band-aid can’t fix
items.summary: India's government and central bank have launched new measures to attract foreign investment. These steps aim to boost the rupee and bring in significant capital for bonds and stocks. However, global challenges like the Iran war and rising costs pose risks. While these actions offer immediate relief, long-term economic health depends on addressing structural issues and global headwinds.

items.title: RBI eases investment norms to deepen foreign participation in equity, debt markets
items.summary: Measures include higher investment limits for overseas investors in listed equities, wider access to government securities and relaxation of FPI debt investment norms

items.title: Jharkhand State Cricket Association signs three-year streaming deal with JioHotstar for JT20 League
items.summary: Jharkhand State Cricket Association has partnered with JioHotstar for digital broadcasting of the Jharkhand T20 League. This three-year deal covers the 2026, 2027, and 2028 seasons. The tournament begins on June 10, 2026. JioHotstar will stream all matches across India. Prominent players are expected to participate. The league aims to nurture future Indian cricketers.

items.title: Bitcoin tumbles after Strategy sale unnerves crypto traders
items.summary: Cryptocurrency is heading for biggest weekly loss since November 2022

items.title: Over 27% Indian households remain offline despite 95% mobile penetration: Report
items.summary: India has achieved widespread mobile phone ownership. However, a significant digital divide persists. More than a quarter of Indian homes lack internet access. This gap now focuses on opportunity rather than just access. Digital skills and gender disparities are key challenges. Future policies must address these issues alongside infrastructure.

items.title: July rollout of India, US trade interim pact likely as two move to seal 'vibrant’ 1st phase: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India and the United States are close to finalizing an interim trade agreement. The first phase of this pact is anticipated to be executed by mid-next month. A high-level US team is scheduled to visit India later this month for further discussions. Negotiations aim to resolve outstanding issues and secure favorable terms for India.

items.title: SEBI clears 5 IPOs to raise over ₹9,900 crore
items.summary: The issues that received SEBI approval includes Truhome Finance, Mehta Hitech Industries and Veegaland Developers

items.title: Hexagon Nutrition IPO: Issue received healthy response on Day 01, booked 1.65 times; GMP signals solid listing
items.summary: Hexagon Nutrition IPO saw strong demand on Day 1, with bids for 3.56 crore shares against 2.16 crore shares offered. NII segment subscribed 2.03 times, retail portion 2.43 times. Company raised ₹41.66 crore from anchor investors. IPO price band set at ₹42-45 per share.

items.title: Rs 5,750 crore Adani block deal: SBI Mutual Fund picks stake from GQG
items.summary: GQG Partners sold stakes in Adani Enterprises and Adani Energy Solutions worth about Rs 5,750 crore through block deals, with SBI Mutual Fund acquiring the shares. The transactions are seen as portfolio rebalancing after a strong recovery in Adani Group stocks over the past year.

items.title: STT Global acquires 3.5-acre land parcel in Navi Mumbai for Rs 282 crore
items.summary: STT Global Data Centres India has bought a 3.48-acre industrial property in Navi Mumbai's Mahape for Rs 282 crore. This strategic acquisition supports the growing demand for digital infrastructure. The site in the TTC Industrial Area offers access to power and connectivity.

items.title: Sumitomo Corporation can invest ¥ 10 trillion in India over the next decade: S Jaishankar
items.summary: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar recently convened with Shingo Ueno, the CEO of Sumitomo Corporation, to explore collaborative investments. Minister Jaishankar expressed optimism about Sumitomo's ability to bolster India's goal of reaching a Y=10 trillion investment target in the coming decade. He commended the company's unwavering support for the 'Make in India' movement.

items.title: Oyo parent Prism Hotels receives Sebi nod for IPO
items.summary: Prism Hotels, Oyo’s parent, received Sebi approval for a Rs 6,650 crore IPO. Targeting a $7–8 billion valuation, the firm will use funds for expansion and strengthening its balance sheet to support its ongoing push toward company profitability.

items.title: Rajesh Exports plunges another 5% despite rejecting ‘scam’ allegations
items.summary: Alleged ₹15.15 lakh crore financial misrepresentation sparks market panic; investors scrutinise institutional giants, including LIC

items.title: For Gen Z diamonds are a personal style statement, not something they can tuck away
items.summary: As Millennials and Gen Z redefine luxury consumption in India, the natural diamond industry is evolving to meet their demand for individuality, authenticity, and everyday relevance. The article explores how brands are reshaping designs, storytelling, and customer experiences to connect with a new generation of diamond buyers who value personal meaning as much as luxury.

items.title: E85 fuel to sell at Rs 20/litre discount to drive ethanol adoption: Oil Minister Hardeep Singh Puri
items.summary: India is turning a new leaf in energy efficiency by rolling out E85 fuel, an environmentally friendly ethanol blend consisting of 85% ethanol, with a significant discount of Rs 20 per litre. This initiative is designed to not just lower fuel prices, but also to lessen India's dependence on imported fuels.

items.title: Migsun Group to invest Rs 250 crore in developing serviced apartment project in Delhi’s Rohini
items.summary: Migsun Group will invest Rs 250 crore to build nearly 500 luxury serviced apartments in Delhi's Rohini. This project will cater to corporate professionals, business travellers, and expatriates. It will offer seamless access to commercial hubs and be about 20 minutes from Indira Gandhi International Airport.

items.title: AI is designing OpenAI's next model in a sign of 'superintelligence': SoftBank's Masayoshi Son to CNBC
items.summary: SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son said his forecast of artificial superintelligence arriving in 10 years was "conservative" and thinks it will be here sooner.

items.title: Broker’s call: Jash Engg (Buy)
items.summary: Systematix

items.title: Pulse of the Street: Markets slip as oil, monsoon worries, RBI's cautious tone weigh
items.summary: Friday’s session captured the nervous mood on Dalal Street: the market opened on a hopeful note, but ended weaker as investors grew cautious amid rising external risks.

items.title: RBI says 4% inflation target not in abeyance; future rate action tied to price persistence
items.summary: Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra confirms the 4 percent inflation target remains unchanged. He raised the FY27 inflation forecast to 5.1 percent due to global factors. Monetary policy actions will depend on inflation becoming broad-based and persistent. The central bank will monitor global supply chain pressures and monsoon impact closely.

items.title: Broker’s call: Honasa Consumer (Buy)
items.summary: ICICI Securities

items.title: Franklin’s Western Asset Management agrees $100mn settlement with SEC
items.summary: Deal with Wall Street watchdog resolves civil investigation as fund manager’s former star trader awaits criminal trial

items.title: CMR Green Technologies IPO: Issue booked 127 times on Day 03; NII, QIB portions see huge demand, GMP signals 40% premium
items.summary: CMR Green Technologies IPO oversubscribed 100 times, attracting strong investor demand across all categories. The IPO, valued at ₹630.88 crore, is entirely an offer for sale with no fresh issue component.

items.title: Wall Street declines as tech rally cools amid strong jobs data, Nvidia falls 3.1%, Broadcom drops 3.9%
items.summary: As of 11:03 a.m. Eastern Time, the S&P 500 fell 1%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.2% and the Nasdaq Composite plunged 1.9%

items.title: RBI fines Canara Bank for violations related to KYC, inactive accounts
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has taken action against Canara Bank, slapping a substantial Rs 41.8 lakh fine for failing to comply with Know Your Customer regulations and for errors in account classification. In a related case, Puran Associates Pvt Ltd has received a Rs 3.1 lakh penalty related to asset classification concerns.

items.title: Govt committed to further drive 'Reform Express', ensure economic momentum: FM Sitharaman
items.summary: India's economic outlook continues to evolve, with Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman highlighting the government's unwavering resolve to enhance economic policies. This declaration aligns with the Reserve Bank of India's recent downward adjustment of the FY27 GDP forecast. In a bid to woo foreign investors, the government has unveiled new tax relief on government securities.

items.title: Tata Trusts defends 1989 Tata Sons share transfer, says Palkhivala vetted deal
items.summary: Suresh Tulsiram Patilkhede, a resident of Mumbai and an independent politician, filed a complaint in the high court and sent notices to the Tata Trustees, which the court subsequently allowed him to withdraw five days later, on 13 May.

items.title: India exempts foreign investors from tax on bonds to shore up rupee
items.summary: Central bank holds interest rates despite pressure on currency from foreign outflows and war in Iran

items.title: Startup Policy Forum submits recommendation to Sebi, finance ministry to ease KYC norms for NRIs investing in India
items.summary: The Startup Policy Forum has urged Sebi and the finance ministry to fix digital onboarding barriers that prevent non-resident Indians from investing in Indian markets remotely.

items.title: WTO sees signs of slowdown in global goods trade growth amid Middle East conflict
items.summary: Despite geopolitical tensions in the Middle East impacting the market, global merchandise trade showcases a surprising resilience, powered by increasing demand for AI-driven electronic components. The World Trade Organization's barometer does point to a looming slowdown in trade growth, but volumes continue to exceed expected trends.

items.title: US stocks today: S&P 500, Nasdaq slides as chip stocks fall, jobs data fuels hawkish Fed fears
items.summary: Markets saw a dip on Friday. Chipmaker stocks weakened after a strong rally. A better-than-expected jobs report fueled expectations of a US Federal Reserve interest rate hike. Investors shifted funds to other sectors. Lululemon Athletica's profit forecast cut also impacted market sentiment. Cooper Companies reported strong results.

items.title: RBI proposes tighter disclosure norms for deposit rates, links bulk deposit pricing to liquidity rules
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is proposing new rules for bank deposit interest rates. Banks will need to publish their rates daily. They can also offer different rates on large deposits depending on how stable the money is. This change aims to make deposit pricing more transparent and linked to liquidity.

items.title: As AI shifts from training to inference, Intel moves up the stack
items.summary: Intel is changing its AI strategy. The company is now focusing on building complete AI systems and infrastructure, not just chips. This move comes as AI applications become more widespread. Intel aims to provide solutions for enterprises deploying AI. Competitors like Nvidia are also emphasizing system-level offerings.

items.title: India’s seafood exports jump 70% in a decade, outpace global trade growth: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India's seafood exports increased by 70 per cent in dollar terms from 2014 to 2025, contrasting sharply with a global international seafood trade expansion of barely 12 to 12.5 per cent during the same period.

items.title: Maruti rebounds with strong market share gains after six-year gap
items.summary: Maruti Suzuki has regained substantial market share after a six-year gap. Strong sales in small cars and SUVs, coupled with new production capacity, have boosted performance. The company's market share rose significantly in recent months. With a healthy booking pipeline and upcoming launches, Maruti Suzuki anticipates further growth and market share gains.

items.title: Musk’s SpaceX lines up retail investors for record IPO allocation
items.summary: Up to a quarter of the rocket builder’s $75bn float will be set aside for individual investors

items.title: Cooperatives key to India’s agricultural growth: NAAS
items.summary: Agricultural cooperatives offer vital solutions for small farmers facing market access and cost challenges, says a research paper.

items.title: The dollar’s decline is overstated — but still real
items.summary: The most reliable measure of dollar dominance is probably the loans and other dollar obligations created outside US

items.title: RBI says fully prepared to tackle ‘Mythos’ cyber threat; advisories issued to banks
items.summary: With the looming cybersecurity risks posed by the Mythos platform, the Reserve Bank of India is taking significant precautions. They've already reached out to various financial entities, sharing essential advisories. Deputy Governor Swaminathan J reassured that they are well-equipped to handle any challenges that arise.

items.title: India can return to 7% GDP growth path in FY28 with macro stability, supply measures: CEA Nageswaran
items.summary: India is on the brink of achieving a robust 7 percent growth in FY28, contingent on maintaining macro stability and ensuring supply chains remain intact. The improvement of external conditions will play a critical role in this outlook. Meanwhile, the Reserve Bank has adjusted its FY27 GDP forecast down to 6.6 percent, grappling with elevated energy costs and supply interruptions.

items.title: Is there an AI stock market bubble, and is it ready to burst?
items.summary: Despite the Iran war, inflation and debt fears, US markets keep hitting record highs, fueled largely by AI. BBC's Samira Hussain looks into whether that bubble will burst.

items.title: Maruti considers shielding small-car buyers from inflation-led price hikes
items.summary: Maruti has implemented a price hike of ₹30,000 from June across all its models due to West Asia war-linked inflation, which has led to a surge in prices of commodities like steel, aluminium, and platinum group metals

items.title: RBI expects healthy foreign capital inflows, better balance of payments after easing measures
items.summary: The RBI is introducing new measures to attract foreign investment. These steps aim to boost capital inflows and improve India's balance of payments. The central bank expects significant overseas funds to enter the country through various channels. These initiatives are designed to make it easier for foreign investors to participate in India's financial markets.

items.title: Quote of the day by Walter Schloss: "If the market’s going wild and you want to be in it, you either have to lower your standards to stay in the game or you buy stuff which may not participate because it’s not part of the game at that time"
items.summary: Legendary investor Walter Schloss highlighted the dilemma of booming markets, where investors face the choice of lowering standards to chase quick gains or sticking to discipline with potentially lagging investments. Maintaining patience and focusing on fundamentals, rather than market fads, is crucial for long-term success and capital preservation.

items.title: Hyundai invites startup applications for Innovation Challenge 2026
items.summary: Hyundai Motor India is seeking innovative startups for its Hyundai Innovation Challenge 2026. This six-month program focuses on advanced solutions in electrification, new mobility, and AI. Startups can apply until June 2026. The challenge encourages groundbreaking ideas beyond the listed focus areas. This initiative aims to foster future vehicle technologies.

items.title: Adani Enterprises, Vodafone Idea among 6 stocks to hit 52-week high, rally up to 40% in a month
items.summary: Even as the Sensex fell 117 points to close at 74,243 on Friday, six BSE 200 stocks touched fresh 52-week highs. Vodafone Idea, Adani Enterprises, CG Power, Polycab India, Adani Energy Solutions and Federal Bank outperformed the broader market, signaling strong investor confidence and continued bullish momentum.

items.title: US stock market today: Nasdaq, S&P 500 futures drop up to 1% as investors rotate out of chip stocks
items.summary: Chip stocks rally stalls, leading to shift in investor focus. US-Iran peace talks and ceasefire in focus. Labor Department to report job gains amid economic uncertainty and high energy prices.

items.title: Stable rates, steady demand: Why real estate players see RBI's pause as a confidence booster
items.summary: With retail inflation easing to 3.48% in April and remaining below the RBI's 4% target, industry stakeholders see little immediate pressure on interest rates, creating a favourable backdrop for the housing market.

items.title: Dow Jones| Nasdaq | US Stock Market Today | Highlights: Nasdaq crashes 1,100 pts, Dow 600 pts as chip stocks slide; jobs data fuels rate hike fears
India, US to execute first tranche of bilateral trade agreement by mid-July, says Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India and the United States will implement the initial part of their trade agreement by mid-July. This deal will provide Indian exporters with better market access compared to competitors. Union Minister Piyush Goyal confirmed the timeline following recent trade discussions. Further high-level meetings are expected soon to finalize the agreement. This development highlights India's growing trade potential.

items.title: Paying beyond disclosed rates to attract deposits not acceptable: RBI Guv Sanjay Malhotra
items.summary: Reserve Bank Governor Sanjay Malhotra stated that paying interest rates higher than disclosed is unacceptable to the central bank. While acknowledging healthy competition for deposits, he stressed the need for transparency from banks. Malhotra also indicated an upcoming updated list of upper layer NBFCs.

items.title: Tata Motors' expansion to go on, Iveco acquisition to help it rank among top 4 global CV makers: N Chandrasekaran
items.summary: Tata Motors is gearing up for an ambitious global journey, aiming to redefine its role in emerging commercial vehicle markets, chairman Chandrasekaran said. With the strategic acquisition of Iveco, the company is setting its sights on breaking into the elite tier of the world’s four largest commercial vehicle manufacturers, he added.

items.title: India posts 7.7% GDP growth in FY26, lifted by strong March quarter
items.summary: Despite the quarterly moderation, the annual figure came in slightly above market expectations for a 7.6% expansion, cushioned by a stronger-than-anticipated January-March performance. The economy had grown 7.1% in 2024-25.

items.title: Airbus delays XLR deliveries to IndiGo as war hits suppliers
items.summary: Deliveries of the A321XLR jets from Airbus are lagging, and IndiGo, a prominent airline in India, is bracing for holdups affecting several of the nine planes it has ordered for this year. The ongoing Middle East conflict is causing supply chain issues that exacerbate the delays.

items.title: UK retail investor demand for SpaceX surges to tens of thousands
items.summary: More than 30,000 small investors register interest in hotly awaited IPO

items.title: Blackstone, CPPIB-backed AirTrunk to invest over $30 bn in India's data centres by 2030
items.summary: Hyperscale data centre operator AirTrunk has announced plans to invest over $30 billion in India by 2030, making it one of the country's largest-ever digital infrastructure commitments. The pledge follows the company's April acquisition of Lumina CloudInfra.

items.title: RBI policy press conference: Here's everything that came up for discussion
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India unveiled new measures to attract deposits and bolster the economy. Governor Sanjay Malhotra discussed strategies for economic growth and inflation management. The central bank aims to draw in foreign exchange through various initiatives. These steps are designed to ensure a stable economic environment and encourage investment.

items.title: India's economy expands at 7.8% over January to March — faster than expected
items.summary: The world's fastest growing major economy, India, has been facing major headwinds due to the conflict in Middle East that began end of February.

items.title: Rupee posts biggest daily gain in 2 months, closes at 94.94 vs USD as RBI ramps up currency defence
items.summary: The rupee gained 0.9% to end at 94.9450 per dollar, its biggest gain since April 2. Forward premiums, ‌the cost of hedging ⁠FX ⁠exposure, plunged to 2.67 rupees, the lowest this financial year, down from 2.85 rupees.

items.title: Stable home loan interest rate to sustain housing demand: Industry bodies CREDAI, NAREDCO
items.summary: The RBI has kept the repo rate steady at 5.25 percent. Real estate industry bodies CREDAI and NAREDCO welcomed this move on Friday. They believe stable interest rates will support housing demand. This decision offers predictability for developers and homebuyers. It aims to maintain market stability amidst global economic challenges.

items.title: IRCTC cracks down on fake accounts, expands AI surveillance in kitchens
items.summary: IRCTC has deactivated millions of suspicious user IDs to combat fraudulent train ticket bookings. This move addresses passenger concerns about ticket availability. Simultaneously, artificial intelligence is now monitoring over 800 railway kitchens. Smart cameras detect hygiene violations, ensuring improved food safety standards for passengers across the Indian Railways network.

items.title: The battle over Ben & Jerry’s gets ugly
items.summary: War of words over ice cream maker’s governance as legal showdown looms

items.title: The Tech Download: Anthropic’s IPO sets up first big test of AI boom valuations
items.summary: Anthropic took a big step this week towards pipping bitter rival OpenAI to a public market listing.

items.title: Rupee rises 81 paise to close at 94.93 against US dollar post RBI policy decision
items.summary: In the interbank forex market, the rupee opened at 95.72, reached a high of 94.89, and closed at 94.93, up 81 paise from its previous close

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex ends 117 pts lower at 74,243; Nifty slips 50 pts to 23,367
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Stock Price Highlights: The benchmark BSE Sensex fell 0.16% to ​74,243.34, while the broader NSE Nifty ‌50 index declined 0.21% to 23,366.7, ​after the Reserve ⁠Bank of India's widely expected rate pause, as investors assessed the central ‌bank's measures to support the rupee against concerns ‌of higher inflation and ‌a lower ⁠growth forecast.

items.title: Why Bata India’s improved revenue growth in Q4 must be taken with a pinch of salt
items.summary: Bata India posted its best revenue growth in three years at 5% in Q4FY26, but the gains were undercut by a 15% Ebitda decline, 2.8% volume growth that trailed peers, and margins that analysts say will stay below pre-covid levels through FY28.

items.title: Weak monsoon may impact rural demand, private consumption: RBI governor Sanjay Malhotra
items.summary: Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra anticipates a potential impact on rural demand and private consumption due to a projected monsoon deficiency. He noted that mitigation measures are in place to lessen these effects. Rising input costs and supply disruptions also pose risks to economic activity, he said.

items.title: India’s GDP expands 7.7% in FY26; Q4 growth at 7.8%
items.summary: India GDP Data Key Insights Q4 FY26: India's economic growth saw a slight dip to 7.8 percent in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2026. The Reserve Bank of India has now lowered its growth projection for fiscal year 2027 to 6.6 percent. This adjustment comes amid increasing global uncertainties. The central bank anticipates varied growth rates across the four quarters of fiscal year 2027.

items.title: India likely to outpace RBI growth forecasts, says World Bank's Neelkanth Mishra
items.summary: India's economy is set for robust growth, exceeding Reserve Bank of India forecasts, World Bank Executive Director Neelkanth Mishra said. According to him, the economy is performing significantly better than official projections. He also supported RBI's decision to maintain interest rates.

items.title: How the Strait of Hormuz standoff flipped the energy security debate
items.summary: Iran's influence over the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz has inadvertently transformed the energy security debate.

items.title: ‘So what? 100 is just a number’: PM Modi's EAC member dismisses alarm over rupee’s potential fall to triple digits
items.summary: Shamika Ravi, a member of the PM's Economic Advisory Council, downplays concerns about the Indian rupee reaching 100 against USD, stating it's just a number. She emphasised managing the economic impact of global supply shocks over exchange rate defence.

items.title: Paras Healthcare IPO: Company files DRHP with SEBI to raise ₹1,800 crore via public issue
items.summary: The proposed IPO comprises a fresh issue of equity shares aggregating up to ₹500 crore and an offer for sale (OFS) of shares worth up to ₹1,300 crore by the selling shareholder, taking the total issue size to up to ₹1,800 crore.

items.title: Sensex ends 117 points lower after RBI policy meet outcome: 10 key highlights from Indian stock market today
items.summary: BSE barometer Sensex closed the day at 74,243, lower by 117 points or 0.16%. Meanwhile, NSE Nifty ended at 23,366.70, down 49.85 or 0.21%.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on June 5: Adani Green, Ather Energy, OLA, CarTrade Tech, YES Bank among top gainers
items.summary: In terms of weekly performance, the Nifty 50 lost 0.8% of its value, extending its losing streak to a second straight week, while the Sensex also closed lower for the second consecutive week.

items.title: OpenAI says it will comply with Trump's order requiring AI model reviews before release
items.summary: George Osborne, OpenAI's Head of Countries told CNBC that governments "have a big role to play in how this technology is used and deployed."

items.title: The Musk exception on banking fees for SpaceX
items.summary: Most companies are still likely to pay full price on smaller US deals as a prestige good

items.title: Top gold ETF managers cap large-ticket inflows. Will it hurt retail investors?
items.summary: HDFC MF's move alone is unlikely to significantly alter industry flows unless other large gold ETF players follow suit

items.title: Who can buy shares in Elon Musk's SpaceX?
items.summary: From next week individual investors can take a stake in Musk's rockets-to-AI company.

items.title: India GDP Highlights: Q4FY26 GDP at 7.8%, real GDP growth at 7.7%, says govt
items.summary: India GDP Highlights: MoSPI releases Q4 FY26 and annual GDP data shortly. Track growth rate, sector performance, comparison with estimates, RBI outlook, and market reaction.

items.title: Rate hikes are coming, RBI has sent a clear signal, says Anubhuti Sahay, Standard Chartered
items.summary: Despite holding the repo rate steady, the Reserve Bank of India's significantly upgraded inflation forecasts signal a strong likelihood of rate hikes from August. Anubhuti Sahay of Standard Chartered highlights that elevated inflation projections, coupled with upside risks from oil prices and El Niño, suggest a deliberate sequencing of policy tools, with further rate increases firmly in view.

items.title: Retail investors' picks: 11 high-margin stocks surge up to 40% in CY26
items.summary: Retail investors increased their holdings in several fundamentally strong companies during the March quarter, even as broader market sentiment remained weak. Among stocks with net profit margins above 10%, 11 companies delivered gains of up to 40% in CY26, reflecting growing retail confidence and resilient business performance.

items.title: RBI governor says no plans to ease net open position restrictions
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India will maintain its restriction on banks' net open positions. Governor Sanjay Malhotra confirmed no plans exist to discontinue the measure. This rule limits overnight unhedged exposure to curb pressure on the rupee. The RBI implemented this at the end of March to stabilize the currency.

items.title: Bluestone eyes 5x rise in revenue as rising affluence to buoy everyday use jewellery buys
items.summary: The jewellery retailer is also converting its existing network of franchise-owned stores to company ownership even as it explores franchise-owned stores to enter small towns.

items.title: Expert view: FII selling more like a stampede towards AI than exodus from India, says Pradeep Gupta of Anand Rathi
items.summary: Expert view: History suggests that foreign flows typically return when valuations become more compelling. That can happen through a market correction, through a period of earnings catching up with prices, or through some combination of the two, says Pradeep Gupta.

items.title: LIC buys Maruti Suzuki shares worth Rs 68 crore, insurer's stake in automaker crosses 5%
items.summary: Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) has increased its stake in Maruti Suzuki above the 5% threshold after purchasing 51,750 shares through a market transaction. The move comes amid a decline in Maruti Suzuki's stock this year and reflects LIC's continued investment activity despite market volatility.

items.title: FII tax exemption in India: Here’s all you need to know
India scraps tax on overseas bond investors in bid to attract foreign capital and shore up the rupee
items.summary: India has announced several measures to boost capital inflows, including the scrapping of capital gains tax for foreign investors in government bonds.

items.title: Meet the big winners of 2026 oil price volatility — and why their strategies are now shifting focus
items.summary: Computer-based trend-following hedge funds have successfully capitalized on huge price moves in energy markets.

items.title: RBI, govt unveil measures to attract foreign inflows in bonds, equities
items.summary: The RBI and government delivered on a coordinated effort towards shoring up capital flows into Indian assets to ease the pressure on the rupee, experts said

items.title: Apollo drops £1.5bn bid for London-listed Bodycote
items.summary: US private equity group will be restricted from making another offer for components maker in coming months

items.title: Meesho share price jumps after falling for eight straight sessions. Choice sees more upside
items.summary: Meesho stock opened at ₹166.46 apiece today, as compared to the previous closing price of ₹165.34 on Thursday. Here's what's driving the stock, along with share price trend.

items.title: Polymer currency notes proposal under consideration, says RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra
items.summary: Speaking at the post-policy press conference on Friday, Malhotra said the consideration was still at a preliminary stage and the central bank was examining its pros and cons.

items.title: Asia tech stocks drop after Broadcom rattles AI trade and drags Wall Street names lower
items.summary: Asian technology shares fell, tracking losses in U.S. semiconductor stocks after disappointing earnings Broadcom sparked a rotation out of AI-linked names.

items.title: Goldman Sachs invests ₹210 crore in Groww; shares rise 4%
items.summary: Goldman Sachs purchased 1,13,43,750 shares of Groww on 4 June at ₹185.50 per share, amounting to ₹210.42 crore. Friale Fund IV LLC, meanwhile, sold the shares of the company. Block deal data was released post-stock market hours on Thursday.

items.title: CMR Green Technologies IPO Day 3: GMP, subscription status, other key details. Should you apply as bidding ends today?
items.summary: CMR Green Technologies IPO price band is set at ₹182 to ₹192 per share, and the IPO lot size is 78 shares. At the upper end of the price band, the company aims to raise ₹630.88 crore from the book-building issue, which is entirely an offer for sale of 3.29 crore equity shares.

items.title: China poaches more AI talent from the U.S. as it eyes the next 'super-app'
items.summary: Tencent Chief AI Scientist Yao Shunyu, who joined the company from OpenAI, said Friday he aims to pursue artificial general intelligence.

items.title: Hindustan Zinc share price tumbles 6% after reports of government's 2% stake sale plan, Vedanta also down 5%
items.summary: Hindustan Zinc fell as much as 5.7% to an intraday low of ₹569.20 on the BSE. Shares of Vedanta, the parent company of Hindustan Zinc, also came under pressure and declined nearly 5% to ₹312.35 apiece following the report.

items.title: Gold futures drop to ₹1,58,011/10g
items.summary: Analysts attribute fall to weak global cues

items.title: Silver futures fall to ₹2.5 lakh/kg
items.summary: Analysts say sell-off by participants mainly weighed on silver prices

items.title: SpaceX won’t make the S&P 500
items.summary: Choose your index wisely

items.title: Blackstone-backed Australian company AirTrunk to invest $30 billion for 5 GW digital infrastructure in India—All we know
items.summary: Blackstone-backed, Australian data centre company AirTrunk plans to invest over $30 billion (more than ₹3,000 billion) to develop over 5 GW of digital infrastructure capacity in India by 2030, the company said.

items.title: India plans up to 2% stake sale in Hindustan Zinc via OFS, may raise $525 million
items.summary: The stake sale is expected to be launched this month or in July as part of the government’s broader asset monetisation programme

items.title: Vedanta Group reports 15% decline in metals emissions intensity since FY21
items.summary: The company’s four-pronged decarbonisation strategy is central to achieving this progress

items.title: JP Morgan gives 'overweight' tag to this Tata Group stock. Do you own?
items.summary: JP Morgan has maintained its ‘overweight’ rating on the jewellery stock, with a target price of ₹5,400 per share. Here's what the global firm has said on the stock outlook.

items.title: Bond yields slip after RBI's FII push: Why India's bond markets could be the biggest winner of this policy?
items.summary: The MPC policy aligned with expectations. Significant measures were announced on foreign capital flows, external borrowing, and forex management. Bond yields softened slightly. Experts caution about inflation outlook.

items.title: We need to stop AI developing without humans, says Anthropic co-founder
items.summary: Jack Clark tells BBC's Newsnight AI could get to the point where it develops without human input.

items.title: HCC bags ₹127 crore Bhutan Hydropower contract, stock edges up
items.summary: The scope of work covers construction of diversion tunnels, hydromechanical gates, and cofferdams

items.title: RBI holds repo rate at 5.25%; markets rise as financials gain, metals drag
items.summary: The MPC raised FY27 inflation projection to 5.1% and trimmed GDP growth to 6.6%, citing global risks including West Asia conflict, crude oil volatility, and rupee weakness

items.title: Actors union approves 4-year contract with studios and streamers
items.summary: Actors' union approves 4-year contract with studios and streamers

items.title: The CPG blues
items.summary: Bad luck and bad decisions in consumer products

items.title: China cracks down on soft porn, violence and materialism in viral micro dramas
items.summary: Micro dramas have surged in popularity, but drawn criticism for often sensationalist content.

items.title: Rajesh Exports falls 10% amid SEBI probe; Rajesh Mehta rejects ₹15.15 lakh cr scam charge
items.summary: SEBI, in its interim order dated June 3, 2026, alleged that Rajesh Exports had prima facie misrepresented nearly ₹15.15 lakh crore in revenues linked to its subsidiaries and step-down subsidiaries between FY21 and FY25

items.title: Hindalco shares slip 1.45% at early trade
items.summary: The stock has returned 74.52% over one year and 24.26% year-to-date, significantly outpacing the Nifty 50

items.title: RBI policy in focus as Sensex, Nifty open higher; Tech and Metal stocks drag
items.summary: Experts expect RBI to hold repo rate at 5.25%

items.title: Stocks to Watch, June 5: Ola Electric, Maruti, Inox Clean, CG Power, TVS SCS, Aurobindo Pharma
items.summary: Corporate India remained active with fundraising, acquisitions, strategic partnerships and capacity expansion, as Ola Electric completed a ₹780-crore QIP, Inox Clean Energy announced a major renewable energy acquisition, and Maruti Suzuki launched India’s first flex-fuel passenger vehicle

items.title: RBI MPC outcome: Central bank slashes FY27 growth projections to 6.6% amid Middle East crisis
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in its June policy meeting on Friday lowered its growth projections for the financial year 2026-27 (FY27) to 6.6% from 6.9% earlier amid the Middle East crisis and elevated crude oil prices along with disruptions to the Southwest monsoon.

items.title: War and weather force RBI into a wait-and-watch mode as risks to growth, inflation loom
items.summary: RBI has trimmed its real GDP growth forecast for FY27 to 6.6% from 6.9% earlier this year, while raising its retail inflation outlook to 5.1% from 4.6%.

items.title: Tata Steel aims to repay overseas debt in two years; cuts FX borrowings
items.summary: Tata Steel's overseas debt stood at ₹16,629 crore at the end of FY26, amounting to 18% of the company's total borrowings of ₹92,382 crore, a company spokesperson told Mint. The figure includes both term debt and working capital borrowings across the company's businesses.

items.title: Wall Street’s red carpet for SpaceX
items.summary: Banks and stock exchanges have bent over backwards for the historic IPO of Elon Musk’s rocket company

items.title: The big backlash over plans to drop US quarterly reporting demands
items.summary: Enraged investors have sent flood of letters to SEC to express their opposition

items.title: Oxford BioMedica leaves door open to private equity takeover
items.summary: UK-listed group has already rejected multiple unsolicited offers from EQT and says a deal would have to be the right fit

items.title: Hedge funds bet against call centre stocks as AI threat grows
items.summary: Outsourcing companies hit as investors see ‘clean’ disruption risk

items.title: How to take advantage of disruption
items.summary: Established industries are facing a fresh wave of digital transformation

items.title: Oil rises as Hezbollah rejects ceasefire, raising fresh fears over West Asia peace
items.summary: Oil prices rose after Hezbollah rejected a US-brokered Israel-Lebanon ceasefire, reviving concerns over wider regional tensions and their impact on global energy supplies.

items.title: AI needs brake pedal: Anthropic's Jack Clark warns AI could soon build itself, says 'I am worried for my kids'
items.summary: Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark warns AI is approaching the ability to build its own successors and calls for a government regulatory 'brake pedal'.

items.title: British Heart Foundation plans to close 150 charity shops
items.summary: The charity says it is facing "an exceptionally challenging trading environment".

items.title: Companies are now cutting worker pay to fund AI investment- Teradata, TTEC slash employee benefits as AI spending surge
items.summary: Teradata has frozen annual salary raises and TTEC has paused retirement benefits to fund AI spending, Business Insider reports. Experts warn more companies could follow.

items.title: India growth story intact, AI not a bubble yet, Citi’s Aloke Gupte says
items.summary: Rupee depreciation is a big factor people consider, because anyone running a global portfolio looks at returns on a dollar basis, not on an individual currency basis, and the rupee's performance impacts what you have invested, Aloke Gupte, Citigroup's co-head of global equity capital markets, said.

items.title: India plays large role in AI era of Windows: president Davuluri
items.summary: Windows chief Pavan Davuluri says India contributed significantly to the operating system’s new agentic AI capabilities and remains a priority market for future growth.

items.title: Google Cloud cuts its own cyber threat hunters as Mandiant layoffs hit security division
items.summary: Google has laid off workers across its Cloud division in June 2026, affecting the Threat Intelligence Group and Mandiant employees, according to Business Insider. Here is what we know.

items.title: Behind Reliance Industries' sale of a step-down unit, an OFCD route
items.summary: A ₹274-crore sale of a third-layer subsidiary by Reliance Industries to a former associate, Jaipur Enclave, in April had set off governance questions on related party transactions. It has now emerged that Reliance lent Jaipur Enclave ₹273.75 crore in convertible debt through another subsidiary.

items.title: Sebi order casts shadow on Rajesh Exports’ battery PLI project
items.summary: Sebi’s observations formed part of a broader order in which the regulator alleged that about 99.8% of revenue—amounting to ₹15.15 trillion—attributed to Rajesh Exports’s subsidiaries between FY21 and FY25 was materially misrepresented

items.title: Trump announces $700m coal investment using wartime powers
items.summary: The president is directing money into coal as the Iran war drives up energy costs for Americans.

items.title: Centre plans to allow PE firms, sovereign wealth funds to directly participate in all greenfield infra projects
items.summary: The Centre is planning to introduce a new public-private partnership framework to allow PE firms, pension funds, sovereign wealth funds and other large financial investors to directly participate in greenfield project development across sectors like power, railways, airports.

items.title: Govt nears a quarter of ₹80,000-cr asset-sale target for FY27
items.summary: The government has raised ₹6,366.93 crore through land monetisation via Infrastructure Investment Trusts (InvITs), while stake sales in Central Bank of India and Coal India fetched another ₹7,808.49 crore.

items.title: The ancient trick making food waste useful and tasty
items.summary: Instead of throwing away byproducts of food processing, fermentation is making them valuable.

items.title: Single 20-somethings need AI to make first move on dating apps - Hinge boss
items.summary: Jackie Jantos says loneliness and lack of confidence were challenges for young adults looking for relationships.

items.title: Mark Zuckerberg's longest-serving employee on AI, jobs - and her boss
items.summary: Naomi Gleit has weathered many controversies at Meta, but remains in what she tells the BBC is her "dream job".

items.title: 'Apocalyptic' Tata Steel fire sees 'substantial' damage to production line
items.summary: Residents are advised to keep windows and doors closed as crews battle the fire in Port Talbot.

items.title: Broadcom suffers $285bn rout as revenue outlook disappoints
items.summary: Chip company notches one of the biggest valuation wipeouts on record as shares drop 12% on Thursday

items.title: Big energy getting bigger
items.summary: NextEra and Dominion announce plans to create a $420bn giant

items.title: AI Isn’t Replacing Credit Hedge Fund Traders Yet, Barclays Says
items.summary: Hedge funds and asset managers are increasingly using artificial intelligence when investing in global credit markets, but the technology isn’t replacing human traders — at least for now, according to a survey by Barclays Plc.

items.title: Flesh-eating screwworm is confirmed in the U.S., officials say
items.summary: The USDA and Texas officials are working to contain New World screwworm from Zavala County, including establishing an infested zone and enforcing quarantine.

items.title: Tech groups find a new way to monetise AI hype: selling volatility
items.summary: Elevated convertible bond issuance is, at heart, a response to the market being twitchier than usual

items.title: Titan bets on premiumisation, global expansion to double business by FY30
items.summary: Titan reported a consolidated revenue of ₹76,078 crore for FY26, up 32.7% from FY25, while net profit surged 52% to ₹5,073 crore. The country's largest jewellery retailer is targeting a two-fold increase in consolidated revenue and operating profit by FY30 compared with FY26 levels.

items.title: Jackson Lab's licence scrapped as probe finds oxytocin jab link to pregnant women's deaths
items.summary: Punjab's drug regulator has scrapped Jackson Laboratories' licence and ordered a nationwide recall after a probe linked its oxytocin injection to the deaths of five pregnant women in Rajasthan. The probe found serious quality lapses, data manipulation and continued production despite stop orders.

items.title: India remains among fastest-growing economies despite global uncertainty: Minister Prasada
items.summary: The minister highlighted India's push in trade, manufacturing, AI and semiconductors at IIFT's Global Business Research Conference.

items.title: All nine recent FTAs set to be operationalized within 10 months, says Goyal
items.summary: India expects the nine FTAs signed over the past three years to be operational within 9-10 months. Minister Goyal says these agreements complement India's growth strategy by expanding market access, facilitating trade an industry, enabling movement of talent and attracting investments.

items.title: Trent looks beyond apparel to focus on beauty, footwear
items.summary: The growing importance of these categories comes as some of Trent's international ventures face challenges.

items.title: Bluesky was launched as a Twitter rival — but it's far less popular. Now it's eyeing Reddit for inspiration
items.summary: "I think the public square is not the direction we want to go in...we're very inspired by companies like Reddit," Bluesky's Rose Wang told CNBC.

items.title: Go Zero ice cream registers 350% growth in May; founder Kiran Shah says, ‘Still not done with the season’
items.summary: Go Zero ice cream registered 350% growth in May, founder Kiran Shah revealed. This shows that health-conscious consumers are seeking alternatives to typical high-fat and high-sugar ice creams in this peak summer season.

items.title: Who is Elon Musk and what is his net worth?
items.summary: The boss of X, Tesla and SpaceX is the world's richest person and has used his platform to make his views known on a vast array of topics.

items.title: Physics Wallah looks to unwind FinZ lending bet days after ₹120 crore infusion
items.summary: The edtech company is exploring sale, loan-book transfer and licence surrender after abandoning plans to lend from its own balance sheet.

items.title: India's viral 'Cockroach' party has millions of followers. What investors need to know
items.summary: A Gen Z-led, social media-first mock political party in India that has amassed millions of followers and is set to hold an in-person protest on Saturday.

items.title: IndiGo pips Air India again on overseas routes as West Asia crisis weighs
items.summary: IndiGo has reclaimed the top spot among Indian carriers on international routes in April, aided by a faster recovery from West Asia-related disruptions than the Air India group. Analysts expect the airline to retain its lead as it expands its overseas network.

items.title: Mint Explainer | Why has the US proposed fresh tariffs on India?
items.summary: Experts have argued that India should not fall for the pressure tactics. It should keep the latest Section 301 tariffs and bilateral trade agreements separate.

items.title: SpaceX says it's worth $1.75tn as it targets largest stock market debut
items.summary: Elon Musk’s space exploration company set a target share price for buyers earlier than expected.
